metadata.title: and most enjoyable events of the

pages.raw_text:
and most enjoyable events of the
week. s-ta---.-'-s


& & & -
The last and one of the most sue
eessful of the winter series of dane
ing parties given by the American
Bankers institute occurred last
evening when they entertained at
the Elks' temple. Arthur Blum, j..
O. Tiffany and Charles Ham had
charge of the committee and sue
ceeded in making the event most
enjoyable. About Is couples at
tended.


Mrs. E. M. Denny entertained the
members of the Central Woman's
Christian Temperance union yester-
day at her home, 1914 Third avenue.
A number of the ladies assisted on
the program and gave interesting
talks along temperance lines.


e & &


The ladies' auxiliary of the
Eastern Star held a social meeting
yesterday afternoon at the parlors
of the Masonic temple. STA '.t


4 4 4


Mr.. and Mrs. Fred B. Grinnell
and Miss Hazel Grinnell left yes
terday for New York. They will
sail January 20 for Europe.


& & & ''ts
Mrs. G.. M. Fassett and daughter,
Miss Kate, left yesterday en- route
for California. After spending a
few weeks at the coast cities they
will return home by way of Ne
vada.


& & &


A jolly crowd of the young people
of the First Evangelical church
spent a most enjoyable time at the
home of Mr.. and Mrs. Ed Kienholz,
E504 Highland avenue, last night.
Games and contests were the
amusements of the evening. The
singing of Mrs. Bertha Darling, who
directs the choir at the church, was
thoroughly appreciated. Refresh
ments were served.


& 4 &


The members of the Roycroft
club held their regular meeting last
evening in the Theosophical club
rooms. Mr.. Benefiel talked on the
subject, Are We Our Own Free
Will Agents?" An interesting dis
cussion followed the address.
